title: knautia rupicola var. macrotrycha
intro: Knautia rupicola var. macrotrycha, also known as the alpine burnet, is a perennial plant in the family Caprifoliaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Knautia rupicola var. macrotrycha include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, and can be used as a tea, tincture, or topical application to harness its medicinal properties.
